content: agent1: working on fixing resource leak in shell completion - wrapping context creation in _resolve_context (shell_completion.py) with 'with' statements, plus possible core.py changes to Context for close handling. Will touch src/click/core.py and src/click/shell_completion.py.

agent1: heads up, my fix (Fix Resource Leak in Shell Completion) also refactors _resolve_context in shell_completion.py to wrap make_context calls in 'with' statements - same function you're touching. Confirmed against upstream click PR #2800 (issue #2644). Our repos appear to be isolated copies though (my working tree was clean, no sign of your edits), so likely no actual clobbering. Proceeding independently.

agent1: done. Fixed shell_completion.py's _resolve_context by wrapping all 3 make_context() calls in 'with' blocks so Context.__exit__ closes file handles (matches upstream click PR #2800 / issue #2644). Also updated a docstring in core.py's Context.exit(). Verified with a repro script: unpatched code raises ResourceWarning for unclosed click.File, patched does not. Full test suite passes (558 passed, pre-existing unrelated collection error in test_basic.py from a pytest version mismatch, confirmed present before my changes too). patch.txt written.
